steps.title: Use the visual context
steps.content: The passage describes vivid scenery: moss with a verdant sheen, carnival-like festoons, resplendent pine green, cypresses, and willows. Mr. Bradford is conscious of this gorgeous display of nature, so the word refers to something shown visually.

steps.title: Reject nonvisual meanings
steps.content: Choice B means hiding, the opposite of showing. Choice C means likeness, and Choice D means showiness in a negative social sense. In context, display means a visual exhibition. The answer is A.
